fields.name: Starter
fields.description: A device used to rotate an internal-combustion engine so as to initiate the engine's operation under its own power.

fields.name: Armature
fields.description: The rotating coil or coils of a dynamo or electric motor.

fields.name: Commutator
fields.description: A rotary switch in certain types of electric motors and electrical generators that periodically reverses the current direction between the rotor and the external circuit.

fields.name: Field Coil
fields.description: An electromagnet used to generate a magnetic field in an electro-magnetic machine, typically a rotating electrical machine such as a motor or generator.

fields.name: Starter Bendix Drive
fields.description: A starter drive which incorporates a one-way clutch and a helical drive spring to engage the engine's flywheel during starting.

fields.name: Alternator
fields.description: A generator that produces alternating current.

fields.name: Rotor
fields.description: The rotating part of an electrical or mechanical device.

fields.name: Stator
fields.description: The stationary part of a rotary system, found in electric generators, electric motors, sirens, mud motors or biological rotors.

fields.name: Housing
fields.description: A durable protective casing or enclosure for an electrical device or system.

fields.name: Flash Relay
fields.description: A relay that interrupts the flow of electrical current at regular intervals to produce a flashing signal.

fields.name: Carbon Brush
fields.description: An electrical contact which conducts current between stationary wires and moving parts, most commonly in a rotating shaft.

fields.name: Hub Bolt
fields.description: A bolt used to secure the wheel hub to the vehicle.

fields.name: T-Bolt
fields.description: A bolt with a T-shaped head that fits into a slot or channel.

fields.name: Washer
fields.description: A flat disk typically used to distribute the load of a threaded fastener, such as a bolt or nut.

fields.name: Nut
fields.description: A fastener with a threaded hole, typically used in conjunction with a mating bolt to fasten multiple parts together.

fields.name: U-Bolt
fields.description: A bolt in the shape of the letter U, typically used to support pipework or as a clamp.

fields.name: Gear
fields.description: A rotating machine part having cut teeth or cogs which mesh with another toothed part to transmit torque.

fields.name: Tie-Rod End
fields.description: A part of the steering mechanism in a vehicle that connects the steering rack to the steering knuckle.

fields.name: Ball Joint
fields.description: A spherical bearing that connects the control arms to the steering knuckles.

fields.name: U-J Cross
fields.description: A universal joint cross, a coupling in a rigid rod that allows the rod to 'bend' in any direction.

fields.name: Brake Hose
fields.description: A flexible hose that carries brake fluid from the brake line to the brake caliper.

fields.name: Fuel Line
fields.description: A hose or pipe used to transfer fuel from the fuel tank to the engine.

fields.name: Engine Mounting
fields.description: A support that secures the engine to the chassis and absorbs vibrations.

fields.name: Support
fields.description: A bracket or fixture used to support or secure an automotive component.

fields.name: Brake Pipe
fields.description: A rigid pipe that carries brake fluid from the master cylinder to the brake calipers or wheel cylinders.

fields.name: Fuel Injection Pipe
fields.description: A pipe used to deliver fuel under high pressure from the fuel pump to the injectors.
